_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-7 Do Not Resuscitate (Revised 11/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-7: Do Not Resuscitate — MDHHS Approved 11/17/2025; MCL 333.1054
purpose: Govern EMS response to patients with valid out-of-hospital Do Not Resuscitate (DNR) orders under MCL 333.1054. A valid DNR combined with absence of vital signs authorizes withholding resuscitation. Patients with vital signs who have a DNR should still be treated per appropriate protocol.

sections.title: Valid DNR Forms — Michigan
points: FOUR types of valid DNR declarations recognized in Michigan:
1. Declarant form — signed by the patient
2. Minor child form — signed by parent or guardian
3. Patient advocate form — signed by legally designated patient advocate
4. Guardian form — signed by court-appointed guardian
All forms must be properly completed and signed to be considered valid
MI-POST form with Section A selecting DO NOT attempt resuscitation is also a valid DNR directive — refer to MI-POST Procedure Protocol

sections.title: DNR Decision Algorithm
points: STEP 1: Assess patient — does patient have vital signs?
STEP 2: If NO vital signs AND valid DNR bracelet or form is present: withhold resuscitation per this protocol (DOA/TOR criteria apply — refer to Dead on Scene Protocol)

STEP 3: If patient HAS vital signs AND has a DNR: treat patient per appropriate treatment protocol; the DNR does not restrict treatment of current condition — it applies only to cardiac/respiratory arrest
STEP 4: If DNR form is invalid, incomplete, or there is doubt about validity: initiate CPR and contact Medical Control
STEP 5: If valid DNR is found AFTER resuscitation has already been started: discontinue resuscitation and contact Medical Control
STEP 6: If DNR is revoked by declarant, patient advocate, guardian, or parent: initiate CPR and contact Medical Control

sections.title: Revocation of DNR
points: A DNR may be revoked at any time by the declarant (patient), patient advocate, guardian, or parent of a minor
Revocation may be verbal, written, or by any action indicating intent to revoke
Upon revocation: initiate resuscitation per appropriate cardiac arrest protocol
Contact Medical Control after initiating resuscitation
Document revocation in PCR

sections.title: Documentation
points: Document presence and type of DNR form or bracelet
Document whether form was valid or invalid
Document patient's vital sign status at time DNR was identified
Document any revocation of DNR
Document Medical Control contact if applicable
Attach or reference DNR form in PCR when possible

deterioration_flags: DNR found but form is invalid or incomplete — initiate CPR; contact Medical Control
Valid DNR found after CPR already started — discontinue resuscitation; contact Medical Control
DNR revoked by patient or authorized person — initiate CPR immediately; contact Medical Control
Patient with vital signs and DNR deteriorating — treat per appropriate protocol; DNR activates only upon arrest
scope_notes: Valid DNR + no vital signs: withhold resuscitation per Dead on Scene / TOR protocol
Valid DNR + vital signs present: treat per appropriate treatment protocol
Invalid/incomplete DNR: initiate CPR and contact Medical Control
DNR found after CPR started: discontinue and contact Medical Control
Revocation: initiate CPR and contact Medical Control
